Metadata.title: Nam's Portfolio | Backend Developer
Metadata.description: Backend Developer specializing in high-performance game server architecture and real-time systems. Explore my projects and technical expertise.

Hero.headline: I design backend systems that stay fast under multiplayer and real-time pressure.
Hero.summary: Backend Developer focused on game server architecture, low-latency messaging, and data paths that hold up under concurrent load.

proofItems.scale: Systems built for thousands of concurrent players
proofItems.availability: Available for freelance and consulting work

runtime.title: Runtime stability first
runtime.description: Designing event flow and service behavior to stay predictable under real-time pressure.

observability.title: Observability built in
observability.description: Logging, alerting, and operational signals are treated as part of the product, not afterthoughts.

delivery.title: Delivery under constraints

About.title: About
About.summaryTitle: Backend engineer with a systems-first mindset
About.summary: I work on backend systems where latency, throughput, and operational visibility directly affect player experience. My strength is turning product requirements into stable runtime behavior, cleaner observability, and infrastructure that remains understandable as it scales.
About.positioning: Best fit for teams building multiplayer, event-driven, or performance-sensitive products.

Skills.title: Capabilities
Skills.intro: The stack matters, but the value is how each layer helps me build fast, stable, and observable systems.

Descriptions.backend: API design, runtime structure, and service boundaries that stay maintainable as product scope grows.
Descriptions.realtime: Connection handling, event flow, and low-latency messaging for interactive systems.
Descriptions.database: Query design, caching strategy, and data access patterns tuned for throughput and response time.
Descriptions.frontend: Enough frontend depth to ship product surfaces, internal tools, and technical demos without handoff friction.
Descriptions.devops: Daily workflow, deployment hygiene, and operational tooling that keep development and debugging efficient.

Experience.title: Experience
Experience.intro: The strongest signal in this portfolio is shipped backend work under live product constraints.

Projects.title: Selected Projects
Projects.intro: A small set of projects chosen to show architecture decisions, delivery ownership, and technical judgment.

Contact.title: Contact
Contact.lead: If you are hiring for backend systems, real-time infrastructure, or performance-focused product work, I am open to a focused conversation.
Contact.directDescription: Email or LinkedIn is the fastest path. The form is available if you prefer async outreach.
Contact.formDescription: Useful for project briefs, collaboration inquiries, or detailed context.
Contact.name: Name
Contact.subject: Subject
Contact.success: Message sent! I'll get back to you soon.
Contact.error: Something went wrong. Please try again.
Contact.availabilityValue: Open to selected freelance, consulting, and recruiter conversations.

Profile.name: Nam
Profile.title: Backend Developer
Profile.tagLine: Specializing in game server architecture, real-time systems, and high-throughput backend engineering.
Profile.availability: Open to freelance projects and consulting opportunities.
Profile.description: Backend Developer with 2+ years of hands-on experience architecting and optimizing game server infrastructure. I build scalable, low-latency systems for products where concurrency, uptime, and response time are not optional.
Profile.contactDescription: Strong fit for teams building multiplayer products, event-driven backends, or systems that need better runtime performance and observability.
highlights: Design backend flows that remain understandable under live product complexity.
Optimize event, socket, and data paths for lower latency and less operational friction.
Bridge implementation and debugging with stronger logging, monitoring, and runtime visibility.

onky.title: Backend Developer (Game Server)
onky.description: Owned backend architecture and runtime stability for multiple online game products, covering server structure, real-time event handling, data performance, and production observability.
onky.scope: Architect and maintain game server infrastructure, optimize live systems, and support feature delivery under multiplayer and operational constraints.
onky.impact: Improved throughput, debugging speed, and system visibility while supporting thousands of concurrent players across production game servers.
achievements: Deployed 20+ game server instances that supported thousands of concurrent players across live products.
Built custom event systems into the core server architecture to support product-specific gameplay logic.
Created an S.O.S monitoring pipeline for real-time alerting, log aggregation, and automated reporting across Telegram, Discord, and Slack.
Reduced database queries by 40% through random distribution algorithm optimization, improving response latency on critical flows.
Refactored the logging layer to enable more granular debugging and faster issue isolation in production.
systems: Real-time gameplay events and socket communication
Game server deployment and runtime orchestration
Database performance tuning for high-traffic workloads

vmu.title: VMU Test Exam
vmu.description: Online exam platform for medical students with real-time admin visibility, question bank management, and exam session support.
vmu.problem: The product needed a stable assessment flow for students plus a moderation layer for admins to monitor live sessions.
vmu.role: Built backend-heavy product flows and real-time session management between user activity and admin tooling.
vmu.impact: Delivered a production-ready learning platform with real-time moderation support and clearer operational visibility during exam activity.

tinylink.title: TinyLink
tinylink.description: URL shortener built as a lean systems exercise around performance, reliability, and observability on constrained infrastructure.
tinylink.problem: Most URL shorteners hide the engineering tradeoffs. This project was built to explore how far a compact service can go with careful runtime and data-path decisions.
tinylink.role: Designed and implemented the service architecture, persistence layer, cache strategy, and deployment path.
tinylink.impact: Showcases engineering judgment around fast redirects, service reliability, and observability in a cost-sensitive environment.

Footer.tagline: Backend developer focused on real-time systems and scalable infrastructure.

V2Meta.title: Thai Thanh Nam | Backend Systems for Real-Time Products
V2Meta.description: Backend developer focused on real-time systems, production reliability, and scalable server-side architecture for multiplayer and operationally sensitive products.

V2Hero.headline: I build backend systems that stay clear, fast, and reliable when concurrency starts to matter.
V2Hero.summary: Backend Developer with 2+ years of hands-on work across multiplayer game servers, real-time communication, monitoring pipelines, and production backend flows. My focus is on runtime behavior that remains understandable under load.

runtime.title: Runtime first
runtime.description: I care about connection flow, concurrency, and service behavior where real-time pressure exposes weak architecture quickly.

observability.title: Observability included
observability.description: Monitoring, alerts, logs, and incident visibility are treated as part of the product, not a cleanup step after shipping.

delivery.title: Delivery with constraints
delivery.description: I aim for maintainable backend delivery that still moves fast under operational limits, legacy constraints, and live product pressure.

experience.label: Designing and maintaining backend systems in production.

servers.label: Real-time multiplayer game server instances delivered with Node.js and WebSocket.

concurrency.label: Systems built to support thousands of concurrent users and operationally sensitive workloads.

incidents.label: Average incident response time improved through internal monitoring and reporting tooling.

stack.label: Practical experience across SQL Server, MySQL, Redis, MongoDB, REST APIs, and real-time messaging.

V2Projects.title: Case studies that show systems thinking, not just feature delivery.
V2Projects.intro: This mix combines production game server work, product delivery, and systems exercises to show how I think about architecture, runtime stability, and operational clarity.

onky.title: ONKY Game Server Systems
onky.summary: Production backend ownership across multiple online game products, including game server architecture, monitoring, live operations, and backend flow optimization.
onky.context: At ONKY, the work involved real-time multiplayer products where service behavior, runtime clarity, and backend stability directly affected live player experience.
onky.build: Built and extended multiplayer game servers with Node.js and WebSocket, added custom game logic on top of a shared framework, designed APIs for internal tools and lobby flows, and shipped internal SOS monitoring and process-management utilities.
onky.systems: Real-time socket communication, master-worker monitoring architecture, Redis-backed message flow, SQL-backed event logic, incident tooling, and operations support for game instances.
onky.impact: Delivered 20+ game server instances, improved average incident response time by 40%, reduced redundant SQL load, and made debugging and rollout operations more predictable in production.

vmu.title: VMU Test Exam
vmu.summary: An online exam platform for medical students with real-time admin visibility, question bank workflows, and backend support for controlled exam sessions.
vmu.context: The product needed a stable testing flow for students while giving administrators enough visibility to monitor sessions and manage learning operations.
vmu.build: Worked on backend-heavy product flows and session support between student activity and admin tooling, helping shape the platform into a usable production-ready learning system.
vmu.systems: Node.js services, Socket.IO-based real-time session visibility, MongoDB persistence, React-based product surface, and operational flows for exam support.
vmu.impact: Turned the system into a more production-ready learning platform with stronger moderation visibility and clearer session handling during live exam activity.

tinylink.title: TinyLink
tinylink.summary: A compact URL shortener built as a systems exercise around performance, observability, and reliability on a constrained stack.
tinylink.context: This project explores the tradeoffs behind a small service that still needs fast redirects, sane data paths, and clean operational behavior.
tinylink.build: Designed the backend architecture, persistence layer, cache strategy, and deployment path with a focus on practical runtime choices rather than abstract scale claims.
tinylink.systems: Fastify runtime, PostgreSQL persistence, Redis caching, Dockerized delivery path, and lightweight observability patterns suitable for a lean service.
tinylink.impact: Shows backend judgment around fast redirects, reliability, and maintainability while keeping the service compact and cost-aware.

V2Capabilities.title: The stack matters, but the real value is what each layer lets me ship confidently.
V2Capabilities.intro: I prefer grouping skills by system capability instead of technology checklists. That maps much better to how backend work is actually delivered.

realtime.title: Event flow, sockets, and interactive runtime behavior
realtime.description: I work well in backend environments where connection handling, messaging timing, event routing, and runtime predictability affect the end-user experience directly.

backend.title: API design and server-side product logic
backend.description: My backend work includes service logic, REST APIs, authentication flows, internal tools, and runtime structures that stay understandable as product scope grows.

data.title: SQL, Redis, and throughput-focused backend decisions
data.description: I optimize data paths where query volume, concurrency, and latency matter. That includes SQL tuning, cache usage, and reducing redundant backend work under load.

observability.title: Monitoring, incident response, and runtime visibility
observability.description: I have hands-on experience building internal monitoring and operational tools that improve alerting, reporting, incident response, and server management in production.

delivery.title: Enough product breadth to ship complete outcomes
delivery.description: Beyond backend-heavy work, I can also contribute across product delivery surfaces when needed, especially for internal tools, admin systems, and full-stack product flows.

V2Experience.title: A progression through production backend work, full-stack delivery, and real-time systems.
V2Experience.intro: The route here matters: internships, product work, freelance delivery, and then live game server systems under real-time constraints.

onky.title: Game Server Developer
onky.summary: Owned backend architecture and runtime stability for multiple online game products, covering server behavior, APIs, data performance, monitoring, and operational tooling.
highlights: Built 20+ real-time multiplayer game servers with Node.js and WebSocket.
Designed internal APIs and backend flows for lobby systems and operational tooling.
Built SOS monitoring infrastructure with Telegram and Slack integrations.
Optimized concurrency and reduced redundant SQL work under load.

onkyIntern.title: Game Server Developer Intern
onkyIntern.summary: Started with low-level real-time backend work and system analysis, proving backend fundamentals through implementation clarity rather than framework abstraction.
highlights: Built a 1v1 Caro game server from scratch using pure Node.js and WebSocket.
Analyzed and documented an existing production game server codebase.
Implemented a new multiplayer game server from company requirements.
Completed internship tasks covering real-time backend and database handling.

freelancer.title: Web Developer
freelancer.summary: Delivered a full-stack learning and testing platform with role-aware access control, course material flows, scoring, and progress tracking.
highlights: Designed and built a full-stack application with React, Express, Node.js, and MongoDB.
Implemented JWT-based authentication and authorization.
Delivered quiz, scoring, progress tracking, and user management features.
Built a separate admin dashboard for users, lessons, and courses.

fostech.title: Web Developer
fostech.summary: Worked on frontend product delivery and integrations, contributing to product management surfaces, reporting flows, and build performance improvements.
highlights: Built UI components and product-management screens for Proshop.
Optimized Webpack configuration to improve load and build performance.
Implemented API integration for dynamic reporting in WLIN CRM.
Supported customer-relationship workflows through React-based interfaces.

fostechIntern.title: Web Developer Intern
fostechIntern.summary: Early product engineering work focused on third-party integration, payment flow correctness, and secure transaction behavior inside an existing React application.
highlights: Integrated the Megapay payment solution into an existing React application.
Implemented authentication and transaction flows from API specifications.
Enabled secure in-app payments for end users.
Completed integration work with production-minded correctness.

V2Principles.title: How I try to build backend systems that survive production reality.
V2Principles.intro: I care less about appearing clever and more about whether the system remains reliable, observable, and understandable once real traffic, incidents, and feature pressure arrive.

reliability.title: Optimize for behavior under pressure
reliability.description: Good backend work should still make sense when concurrency rises, incidents happen, or a live product starts exposing weak assumptions.

clarity.title: Keep runtime and code paths understandable
clarity.description: I value systems that are easier to inspect, debug, and extend instead of architectures that only look impressive on paper.

concurrency.title: Design with load and timing in mind
concurrency.description: Connection handling, event flow, redundant queries, and race conditions are not edge cases in real-time products. They are the product.

visibility.title: Observability is part of delivery
visibility.description: Monitoring, alerts, logs, and reporting should reduce operational stress and shorten incident response time, not just decorate the stack.

V2Contact.title: If you need backend ownership around real-time systems, runtime clarity, or production stability, let's talk.
V2Contact.intro: Best fit for teams building multiplayer products, internal operational tooling, event-driven backends, or systems where observability and reliability are part of the product.
V2Contact.availabilityValue: Open to selected freelance, consulting, and recruiter conversations with a strong backend or systems angle.
V2Contact.educationValue: Bachelor of Engineering in Computer Science (Information Technology), Ho Chi Minh City University of Technology and Education.
